under_20_miles.description: One-way fare for trips up to 19.9 miles

over_20_miles.description: One-way fare for trips 20 miles or more

santa_clarita_antelope_valley.description: One-way fare for trips within Santa Clarita Valley or Antelope Valley

standard.transfer_trips: Same distance-based rates apply to transfer trips

pca.description: Personal Care Attendants ride free. Must be identified when making reservation. Must board and exit with rider.

guest.description: One guest always allowed if space available. Additional guests permitted only if room exists. Guests pay same fare as rider.

children.6_and_over: Pay fare as guest, need own seat
children.car_seat_required: Children under 8 or under 4'9" must use safety/booster seat. Rider provides seat; driver may assist installation.

payment_methods.note: Exact change only. Drivers cannot make change.
Not accepted in Santa Clarita region
Non-refundable. Access not responsible for lost or stolen coupons.

plus_zone.use: For trips 20+ miles (use with base fare coupon)

flex.use: For Santa Clarita/Antelope Valley trips, or $2.00 toward any trip

purchase_options.instructions: Write Rider ID on check/money order. Send to PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734. Allow 10 days for processing.
Order through accessla.org coupon ordering page

locations: El Monte office ($500+ purchases only, by appointment)

advance_booking.same_day: Not guaranteed, may be available based on capacity

reservation_hours.general: 6:00 AM to 10:00 PM, 7 days a week
reservation_hours.santa_clarita_antelope_valley: Monday through Saturday 6:00 AM to 8:00 PM, Sunday 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM

booking.one_hour_window: Reservationists may offer pick-up times up to one hour before or after your requested time. If exact time unavailable, two alternatives are provided.

online_booking.northern_region: Northern region uses WMR app only

booking.return_trips: Must be explicitly requested. Scheduling is NOT automatic.
booking.modifications: Call reservations as soon as possible. Same-day changes may not be possible due to vehicle assignments.

standing_orders.description: Recurring trips for consistent schedules (work, school, church, dialysis)
standing_orders.different_times_allowed: Different pickup times allowed on different days
standing_orders.cancellation_rule: Canceling 30+ consecutive days permanently cancels standing order

on_time_window.description: Vehicle is considered on-time if it arrives within 20 minutes of your scheduled pickup time. Example: if scheduled for 10:00 AM, vehicle may arrive between 9:40 AM and 10:20 AM.

driver_wait_time.description: Driver waits 5 minutes after arrival at the curb. If driver arrives early, the 5-minute wait begins at your scheduled pickup time.

call_outs.description: Automated phone/text notification when your vehicle is approaching
call_outs.note: Cannot be guaranteed. May fail with incorrect phone numbers, voicemail systems, or devices requiring extra key presses. Cell phone numbers recommended.

pickup.vehicle_recognition: White minivans/sedans with Access logo on doors, or certified Access taxis with logos on sides/rear. Ask driver if uncertain.
pickup.eta_requests: Call 800-883-1295 (press Option 4) within 15 minutes of your scheduled pickup time. You'll receive the vehicle number, type, location, and arrival estimate.

wheres_my_ride.description: Track vehicle location 15 minutes before scheduled pickup

pickup.missed_trip: Call Operations Monitoring Center (OMC) at 800-827-0829 (press 2). Available 24/7. OMC secures alternate Access vehicle or backup provider.

curb_to_curb.description: Access is a curb-to-curb service. Driver cannot enter buildings or private driveways.
curb_to_curb.rider_responsibility: Rider is responsible for reaching the curb. Driver offers boarding assistance but does not lift or carry riders.
curb_to_curb.unsafe_locations: Vehicles cannot access unsafe locations. Driver finds closest safe alternative.
curb_to_curb.parking_rules: Drivers avoid blocking driveways, emergency entrances, and red-zoned areas.

definition: Canceling less than 2 hours before scheduled pickup time
Driver arrives within 20-minute window, waits 5 minutes, cannot find you, and obtains dispatcher approval to depart
not_a_no_show: Driver arrives after the 20-minute window and rider cancels
Driver arrives or departs before the scheduled pickup time
Driver fails to wait the required 5 minutes

threshold.description: Five or more no-shows in a calendar month exceeding 10% of your total scheduled trips triggers potential suspension

consequences.clearance: Suspension record clears after 6 months without additional violations

no_show.appeal: Contact Customer Service to explain circumstances or dispute decisions
prevention_tips: Keep a reliable phone number on file
Be ready and visible at the curb before pickup time
Use the Where's My Ride app to track your vehicle
Ask family or caregivers for help with reminders

cancellation.advance_required: At least 2 hours before scheduled pickup time
cancellation.late_cancellation: Canceling less than 2 hours before pickup is recorded as a No Show
cancellation.drop_off_pickup: Schedule as two separate trips. Driver cannot wait at a location.

mobility_devices.weight_includes: Combined weight of device AND rider
mobility_devices.vehicle_types: Accessible vehicles have ramps; some have lifts
mobility_devices.securement: Positioning belts offered for mobility device security. Seatbelts required (extensions available).
mobility_devices.device_change_notice: Riders changing devices must notify Access Customer Service to ensure proper vehicle assignment
mobility_devices.device_users: Riders using wheelchairs/scooters automatically receive accessible vehicles
mobility_devices.non_device_users: Non-device-using riders may receive standard or accessible vehicles (cannot request specific types)

pca.requirements: Must be identified at time of reservation. Must board and exit with rider. Cannot be required but recommended for riders with cognitive conditions.
pca.how_to_add: Call 800-827-0829 to add PCA to your card
pca.finding_aide: Personal Assistance Services Council (PASC) at 877-565-4477 can help locate aides

guests.limit: One guest always allowed if space available. Additional guests only if room exists.

children.under_5_requires_adult: Children 5 and under require accompanying adult
children.car_seat_required: Ages 5-8 or under 4'9" must use safety/booster seat (rider provides; driver may assist installation)

required_behaviors: Headphones for audio equipment (exception: communication devices for hearing/speech impaired)
prohibited: Harassment of riders, drivers, or employees
Riding under alcohol or drug influence
code_of_conduct.consequences: Violations may result in suspension. Physical assault or dangerous behavior may trigger immediate suspension and criminal prosecution.

penal_codes.245.2: Assault with deadly weapon on transportation worker
penal_codes.penalties: Fines up to $10,000 and up to five years imprisonment

code_of_conduct.involuntary_behavior_exception: Non-violent involuntary behavior without direct threat does not result in suspension
code_of_conduct.video_audio_recording: All vehicles equipped with cameras. Occupants are subject to recording for safety purposes.

how_to_file.mail: Access Customer Service, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91734

required_information: Names of involved parties and witnesses
Detailed summary (including pickup address, requested time, given time, actual arrival for pickup issues)
complaints.rider360_tracking: Registered web users can view complaint history and file new complaints at accessla.org/rider360

federal_escalation.agency: Federal Transit Administration, Office of Civil Rights
federal_escalation.address: Attention: Complaint Team, East Building, 5th Floor TCR, 1200 New Jersey Ave SE, Washington, DC 20590

complaints.retaliation_protection: Riders may not experience retaliation for complaints. Access immediately investigates retaliation claims.

title_vi.filing_deadline: Within 180 days of the alleged discriminatory act
title_vi.format: Written and signed by complainant or representative, detailing facts and circumstances

timeline.final_decision: Within 90 days (Executive Director provides written decision)

title_vi.appeal: Dissatisfied complainants may appeal to Federal Transit Administration Office of Civil Rights

eligibility.basis: Based on the person with a disability's ability to use accessible buses and trains in LA County, not solely based on disability, age, or medical diagnosis.

categories.description: Eligible for certain trip types based on functional limitations

step_1.name: Determine Eligibility
step_1.action: Download Evaluation Information and Application Packet from accessla.org (English/Spanish). Call 800-827-0829 for alternative formats.

step_3.name: Schedule Transit Evaluation
step_3.languages: English and Spanish. Sign language interpretation available upon request.
step_3.transportation: Free transportation to/from evaluation center provided if needed
step_3.duration: Process takes up to 4 hours total. Evaluation itself is approximately 45 minutes.
bring: Medical documents, reports, medication list explaining disability impact
Mobility device normally used in community

step_4.name: Approval Process
step_4.delayed: Service may be used pending notification if delayed beyond 21 days

renewal.notice: Renewal information mailed 60 days before expiration
renewal.action: Ensure address and phone are current. Contact Customer Service at 800-827-0829 (Option 3) if notice not received.

appeal.how: Request an appeal by calling Customer Service at 800-827-0829. Appeals form available in English/Spanish.

id_card.description: Green Access Rider ID Card with ID number below name. Must be shown for every trip.
id_card.fraud_warning: Cards cannot be shared. Sharing constitutes fraud punishable by criminal prosecution and eligibility loss.
id_card.lost_card: Call 800-827-0829 (Option 4) to authorize riding with valid government ID while waiting for replacement.

eligibility.temporary_disability: Yes, you can apply for Access with a temporary disability. Call Customer Service at 800-827-0829.

packages_luggage.limit: Two large paper grocery bags per rider (including PCA and/or guest)
packages_luggage.storage: Packages should be stored in available storage area
packages_luggage.exceptions: Approved if slight excess won't inconvenience other riders
packages_luggage.rider_responsibility: Rider maintains control of packages during travel

service_animals.carrier_not_required: Service animals are not required to be in carriers
service_animals.non_service_pets: Must be in secured carriers. Driver cannot assist loading/unloading.

lost_and_found.hours: 8 AM to 5 PM, 7 days a week
lost_and_found.disclaimer: Access not responsible for lost or damaged items

tipping: Drivers are prohibited from accepting tips. Compliments can be sent through Customer Service.
language_access: When calling Reservations or Customer Service, let the agent know which language you speak. They will connect you with a translator who can support throughout the call.

holiday_schedule.description: Access Services observes the following holidays. Standing orders are automatically cancelled on these days. Regular next-day reservations may still be available depending on the region.
holiday_schedule.note: Service availability varies by region on holidays. Call Reservations at 800-883-1295 to check holiday service in your area.

public_records_request.description: Members of the public may request copies of Access Services public records.
public_records_request.how_to_submit: All requests must be made in writing using the Public Records Request Form available on accessla.org.

submit_to.mail: Access Services, Public Records Unit, PO Box 5728, El Monte, CA 91731

public_records_request.guidelines: See Public Records Act Guidelines document on accessla.org for detailed policies.

rider_alerts.description: Access Services publishes rider alerts for service changes, disruptions, and important notices.
how_to_access: Subscribe to RSS feed for automatic updates
Call InfoLine at 213-270-6110 for recorded service announcements
Sign up for email alerts via Constant Contact on the Contact Us page
